instruction: Should I keep this opening hand on the play: Underground Sea, Polluted Delta, Brainstorm, Ponder, Force of Will, Daze, Murktide Regent?
output: This is a strong keep. You have 2 lands including a fetchland for Brainstorm shuffling, both cantrips to find more lands and threats, Force of Will plus Daze for free interaction, and Murktide Regent as a win condition. The hand has everything Dimir Tempo wants: mana, cantrips, disruption, and a finisher. On the play, Daze is at its best. Play Underground Sea, cast Ponder to set up your next draws. This hand is a 9/10.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand on the draw: Island, Wasteland, Force of Will, Force of Will, Daze, Daze, Orcish Bowmasters?
output: This is a risky but keepable hand. You have 4 pieces of interaction but only 1 colored mana source and 1 Wasteland (colorless). You need to draw a second blue source to cast Bowmasters and the second Daze. However, Force of Will and Daze protect you through turn 2, and if you draw any blue land or fetchland, the hand is excellent. On the draw, you get an extra card. Mulligan if you are risk-averse, keep if you trust your draws. This is a 6/10 hand.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand: Dark Ritual, Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal, Lion's Eye Diamond, Burning Wish, Rite of Flame, Ponder?
output: This is a snap keep in The EPIC Storm. You can win turn 1: Ponder to set up your library (or skip it), Lotus Petal for R, Rite of Flame for RR, Dark Ritual for BBB, Dark Ritual for BBB, Lion's Eye Diamond (crack in response to Burning Wish for BBB), Burning Wish for Tendrils of Agony. That is 7 spells cast, so Tendrils with storm 7 drains for 16. You might need to sequence slightly differently, but this hand kills turn 1 through anything except Force of Will. Absolute snap keep, 10/10.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand: Plains, Plains, Plains, Thalia Guardian of Thraben, Stoneforge Mystic, Flickerwisp, Swords to Plowshares?
output: This is a good keep for Death and Taxes. You have 3 lands to curve out, Thalia on turn 2 (taxes all their spells), Stoneforge on turn 2 (tutors Batterskull), Flickerwisp for value, and Swords for removal. The only concern is no Aether Vial (your best card) and no Wasteland or Rishadan Port for mana denial. But the creature curve is strong and Thalia on turn 2 against blue decks is game-warping. Keep. 7/10.

instruction: Should I keep this opening hand: Volcanic Island, Scalding Tarn, Delver of Secrets, Delver of Secrets, Lightning Bolt, Lightning Bolt, Lightning Bolt?
output: This is a trap hand. It looks aggressive but it is actually terrible. Two Delvers with no cantrips means you have no way to set up Delver flips and they will stay 1/1s. Three Bolts with no Force of Will or Daze means any combo deck kills you before your Bolts matter. You have zero card selection to improve the hand. Against combo: you lose. Against fair decks: you probably still lose because Delver does not flip and Bolt only does 9 damage total. Mulligan to 6. 3/10.

instruction: Should I mulligan this 6-card hand: Polluted Delta, Underground Sea, Brainstorm, Thoughtseize, Force of Will, Orcish Bowmasters?
output: Keep this 6-card hand immediately. It has everything: 2 lands including a fetchland, Brainstorm for card selection, Thoughtseize for proactive disruption, Force of Will for free countermagic, and Bowmasters as a versatile threat. This 6-card hand is better than most 7-card hands. Thoughtseize turn 1 takes their best card, Bowmasters turn 2, then Brainstorm with fetchland to find more gas. 9/10 for a mulligan to 6.

instruction: Board state: I have Murktide Regent (8/8 flyer), opponent has Marit Lage token (20/20 flying indestructible). Who is winning?
output: Your opponent is winning. Marit Lage is a 20/20 flying indestructible token that kills in one attack. Your 8/8 Murktide Regent cannot block it (both have flying, but Marit Lage has 20 power to your 8 toughness so it kills Murktide and still deals 12 to you). You need to either: 1) Race by attacking with Murktide and removing their blockers (you need 3 attacks, they need 1). 2) Bounce Marit Lage with Karakas (if legendary - it is not, so this does not work). 3) Use Swords to Plowshares or similar exile effect. 4) Counter the Dark Depths or Thespian's Stage activation before it creates the token. If the token exists and you have no exile removal, you have approximately one turn to win.

instruction: Board state: I have Thalia Guardian of Thraben, my opponent has 3 cards in hand and 2 Islands untapped. Should I play my Stoneforge Mystic?
output: Yes, you should play Stoneforge Mystic. Your opponent has 2 Islands untapped. With Thalia in play, their counterspells cost 1 more. Force of Will normally costs a pitch card and 1 life, but with Thalia it costs a pitch card, 1 life, AND 1 mana. They have 2 mana, so they CAN pay for Force. However, if they Force your Stoneforge, they spend 2 cards (Force + pitch) to stop your 2-drop. That is a good trade for you since you still have Thalia attacking. If they do not counter, you get Batterskull. Play Stoneforge. Either outcome is favorable.

instruction: Board state: My opponent played Show and Tell. I have Containment Priest in hand. When do I put it into play?
output: Put Containment Priest into play with Show and Tell itself. Show and Tell says each player MAY put a permanent from their hand into play. Containment Priest enters the battlefield first (simultaneously with their creature). Containment Priest says: if a nontoken creature would enter the battlefield and it was not cast, exile it instead. Since their Emrakul or Atraxa is entering via Show and Tell (not being cast), Containment Priest exiles it. This is one of the best Show and Tell counter-strategies. The timing works because both permanents enter simultaneously and Containment Priest's replacement effect applies.

instruction: Board state: I have Painter's Servant naming blue in play. My opponent casts Abrupt Decay targeting it. Can I counter with Red Elemental Blast?
output: No, you cannot. Abrupt Decay says 'this spell can't be countered.' Red Elemental Blast says 'counter target blue spell' and with Painter naming blue, Abrupt Decay is indeed blue. However, Abrupt Decay's 'can't be countered' clause overrides your ability to counter it. Your Red Elemental Blast would be a legal target (it targets a blue spell), but on resolution it would fail to counter Abrupt Decay. Your Painter's Servant will be destroyed. You need to protect it with a different method, such as giving it hexproof or bouncing it in response.

instruction: Board state: I control Blood Moon. My opponent plays a fetchland. Can they activate it?
output: Yes, they can activate the fetchland, but it will not do what they want. Under Blood Moon, their fetchland enters the battlefield as a Mountain (it loses all abilities except being a Mountain). However, if they ALREADY had the fetchland before Blood Moon resolved, it is now a Mountain with no activated abilities. They cannot sacrifice it to search. If they play a new fetchland after Blood Moon is in play, it enters as a Mountain immediately and cannot be activated. The key timing is: fetchlands already in play when Moon resolves lose their abilities. New lands enter as Mountains.

instruction: Board state: I have Delver of Secrets (1/1) and Wasteland. Opponent has Underground Sea and Tropical Island. Should I Wasteland now or wait?
output: It depends on what your opponent's deck is doing. If they only have these 2 lands and no other mana sources, Wasteland the Underground Sea NOW. This cuts them off black mana entirely (Tropical only produces UG), stranding Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Bowmasters in hand. If they have a fetchland or other blue source, wait - they can still cast blue spells and your Wasteland is better saved for a utility land like Urza's Saga. The general Wasteland heuristic: destroy the land that cuts them off a color, or destroy the most impactful utility land. Do not Wasteland a basic.

instruction: Board state: Both players at 10 life. I have 2 Lightning Bolts in hand, opponent has Force of Will in hand (I saw it from Gitaxian Probe). Can I win?
output: You have 6 damage from 2 Bolts, but opponent is at 10 life, so you need 4 more damage from somewhere. However, if they Force of Will one Bolt, you only deal 3 damage and they pay 1 life for Force (going to 9). Key question: do they have a blue card to pitch? If yes, they can Force one Bolt and you deal only 3. If their Force of Will is the only blue card, they cannot use it. The line: cast Bolt targeting them. If they Force, they go to 9 (10 - 1 life for Force). Then cast second Bolt for 3 (they go to 6). Not lethal either way from 10. You need another source of damage first.

instruction: Board state: I have Chalice of the Void on 1. Opponent casts Force of Negation pitching a blue card (alternative cost). Is Force countered?
output: No, Force of Negation is NOT countered by Chalice on 1. Chalice of the Void counters spells with the specified mana value. Force of Negation has a mana value of 3 (its printed mana cost is 1UU = 3), regardless of how it was cast. The alternative cost of exiling a blue card changes how you pay for it but does NOT change its mana value. Chalice on 1 only counters spells with mana value 1. To counter Force of Negation with Chalice, you would need Chalice on 3. Same applies to Force of Will (mana value 5) - Chalice on 1 does not stop it.

instruction: Board state: I have Leyline of the Void in play from the start of the game. Opponent casts Reanimate targeting a creature in their graveyard. What happens?
output: Reanimate cannot target a creature in your opponent's graveyard because there are no creatures in their graveyard. With Leyline of the Void in play, cards that would go to your opponent's graveyard go to exile instead. This means creatures that die, are discarded, or are milled are all exiled. When your opponent tries to cast Reanimate, there are no legal targets in their graveyard. Reanimate requires a target on cast, and without one, it cannot be cast at all. This is why Leyline of the Void is the #1 sideboard card against Reanimator.

instruction: Board state: Opponent has Emrakul, the Aeons Torn in play via Sneak Attack. It is their combat phase. I have Karakas untapped. Can I bounce Emrakul?
output: Yes! Karakas can bounce Emrakul. Karakas says 'tap: return target legendary creature to its owner's hand.' Emrakul has protection from colored spells, but Karakas is a land ability, not a spell. Protection does not prevent abilities of permanents from targeting or affecting a creature. Activate Karakas before combat damage is dealt (during the declare attackers step or declare blockers step). Emrakul returns to hand, no combat damage is dealt, and the annihilator trigger has already happened if you waited until after attackers. To avoid annihilator entirely, bounce BEFORE they declare attackers in the beginning of combat step.
